message:
Add quota status robot command

New:
- robot/quota_status.go: Quota status reporting

Beads tracking updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3h0n","title":"Task: Create timeline panel widget for dashboard","description":"## Overview
Implement the visual timeline panel that renders agent activity as horizontal bars.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/tui/dashboard/panels/timeline.go:
   - TimelinePanel struct implementing Panel interface
   - Horizontal bars per agent showing state over time
   - Color coding: Idle=gray, Working=green, Waiting=yellow, Error=red
   - Time axis at bottom with tick marks

2. Rendering logic:
   - Calculate pixels per time unit based on panel width
   - Render each agent as a horizontal track
   - Fill bars with state colors for each time period
   - Add labels for agent names on left

3. Visual design:
   Agent-1: [████░░░████████░░███████░░░░░░████]
   Agent-2: [░░░░████████░░░░░░░░████████░░░░░░]
   Agent-3: [████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░███████]
            |-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|
            10:00 10:15 10:30 10:45 11:00 11:15

4. Panel controls:
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys
   - Scroll left/right with arrow keys
   - Jump to now with 'n' key
   - Show event details on hover/select

## Implementation Notes
- Use lipgloss for colored block rendering
- Efficient rendering (only render visible time window)
- Support variable panel width

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timeline renders correctly in dashboard
- Colors match state semantics
- Zoom and scroll work smoothly
- Panel adapts to width changes
- Unit tests for rendering logic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:50.199826149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:52:03.473303629Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:52:03.473236923Z","close_reason":"Timeline panel implemented: TimelinePanel struct with horizontal bars per agent, color coding (Working=green, Waiting=yellow, Error=red, Idle=gray), time axis with tick marks, zoom (+/-), scroll (arrows), jump to now (n), details (enter). 14 unit tests pass.  {"id":"bd-3h8q","title":"E2E Tests: YAML config driven swarm startup","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of starting swarm from YAML config file.

## Test Scenario
1. Create swarm.yaml with full config
2. Run ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml
3. Verify agents match config
4. Verify prompts match config
5. Verify assignment settings applied

## Test Config (test_swarm.yaml)
```yaml
session: e2e-config-test
agents:
  claude: 2
  codex: 1
options:
  no-user: true
prompts:
  init: "Test initialization prompt"
assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 3
```

## Verification
- Session name matches config
- Agent count matches config
- Init prompt sent to all agents
- Assignment limit respected

## Command: --robot-quota-status

```bash
ntm --robot-quota-status
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "quota": {
    "last_updated": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    "providers": {
      "claude": {
        "usage_percent": 45,
        "requests_used": 450,
        "requests_limit": 1000,
        "tokens_used": 45000,
        "tokens_limit": 100000,
        "cost_usd": 12.50,
        "reset_at": "2024-01-16T00:00:00Z"
      },
      "openai": {
        "usage_percent": 80,
        "requests_used": 800,
        "requests_limit": 1000,
        "tokens_used": 160000,
        "tokens_limit": 200000,
        "cost_usd": 8.00,
        "reset_at": "2024-01-15T12:00:00Z"
      }
    },
    "total_cost_today_usd": 20.50,
    "total_cost_month_usd": 150.00
  }
}
```

## Command: --robot-quota-check

```bash
ntm --robot-quota-check claude
```

Returns just the specified provider's data.

## Implementation
Fetch from caut cache (populated by poller) and format as JSON.

 {"id":"bd-3hrk","title":"EPIC: Agent-to-Agent Communication via Agent Mail Threads","description":"**IDEA #6 - AUTONOMOUS COLLABORATION (82% confidence)**

## What It Does
Enable agents to directly message each other through Agent Mail threads, allowing structured collaboration without human intermediation. Agents can ask questions, share findings, request reviews, and coordinate work autonomously.

## Why This Matters
- Currently agents work in isolation; coordination requires human relay
- This enables autonomous multi-agent collaboration
- Particularly valuable for large refactors, code reviews, parallel feature work

## How It Works
Automatic thread creation when agents are spawned:
- NTM creates a project-wide coordination thread
- Registers each agent with Agent Mail using adjective+noun names
- Periodically injects inbox summaries into agent context
- Surfaces urgent messages immediately
- Tracks acknowledgments

## Agent Communication Patterns
1. Status broadcasts: 'I have completed the auth module, moving to tests'
2. Questions: 'BlueLake: Does anyone know the expected return type for getUserById?'
3. Handoffs: 'GreenCastle: @RedStone I have finished the API, ready for your review'
4. Conflict alerts: 'Warning: I am about to modify handler.go which BlueLake touched 5m ago'

## Robot Mode Support
ntm --robot-mail-check=myproject --agent=cc_1
Returns unread count, urgent count, and message previews with suggested actions

## Flywheel Alignment
- Agent Mail was built for exactly this purpose
- Makes the 'mail for agents' metaphor concrete
- Creates audit trail of agent decisions and coordination

## Technical Implementation
- On spawn: registerAgentsWithMail() in spawn.go
- Dashboard integration: checkAgentInboxes() periodic polling
- Robot flag: --robot-mail-check for AI orchestrators

## Dependencies
Depends on: ntm init, Auto File Reservation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:23:04.448344612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:22.483198453Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hrk","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:22.483153508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3hxdl","title":"Task: Add last_output_ts field to pane status","description":"## What

Add `last_output_ts` field tracking when the pane last produced output.

## Why

Timeout-based stuck detection needs to know when output last changed.
"No output for 5 minutes" is a strong signal the agent is stuck.
Currently this requires external state tracking.

## Implementation

```go
type PaneOutputTracker struct {
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    lastOutput  map[int]time.Time  // pane_index -> last output time
}

func (t *PaneOutputTracker) Update(paneIndex int) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastOutput[paneIndex] = time.Now()
}

func (t *PaneOutputTracker) Get(paneIndex int) time.Time {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    return t.lastOutput[paneIndex]
}
```

## State Persistence

- Option A: In-memory only (reset on ntm restart) - simpler
- Option B: Persist to session metadata file - survives restarts

Recommend Option A for MVP, add persistence later if needed.

## How to Detect Output Changes

During status gathering, compare current pane content hash with previous.
If changed, update timestamp.

```go
func checkOutputChanged(pane int) bool {
    current := capturePaneContent(pane)
    hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(current))
    if hash != lastHash[pane] {
        lastHash[pane] = hash
        return true
    }
    return false
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T23:15:00Z",  // ISO 8601
    "seconds_since_output": 312                 // Convenience field
  }]
}
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Mock time, verify timestamp updates
- Integration test: Verify with actual pane output changes","notes":"Feature already implemented in status_enrich.go. getLastOutput function tracks pane output timestamps and enrichAgentStatus populates LastOutputTS and SecondsSinceOutput fields correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:23.314896135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:38:40.617201274Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:38:40.617114601Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hxdl","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:23.343786489Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3id8q","title":"xf: Add [integrations.xf] config section","description":"Add configuration support for xf integration.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.xf]
# Enable xf knowledge search
enabled = true

# Path to xf binary
binary_path = ""

# Default search mode: lexical, semantic, or hybrid
search_mode = "hybrid"

# Maximum results to return
max_results = 10

# Content types to search
content_types = ["tweet", "reply", "dm"]

# Date range filter (empty = all time)
since_date = ""
```

## Struct Definition

```go
type XFConfig struct {
    Enabled      bool     `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath   string   `toml:"binary_path"`
    SearchMode   string   `toml:"search_mode"`
    MaxResults   int      `toml:"max_results"`
    ContentTypes []string `toml:"content_types"`
    SinceDate    string   `toml:"since_date"`
}
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:43:03.737214850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:43:03.766575526Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3id8q","depends_on_id":"bd-z8gbe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:43:03.766538937Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
